title: graphistylis dichroa
intro: Graphistylis dichroa is a plant species commonly known as the 'yellow flag' or 'yellow flagroot' and is native to the mountainous regions of Australia and New Guinea. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, and has been used as a natural remedy to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Graphistylis dichroa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
